calendar.matters:
Default Prove-Up Hearing.
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

James M. Barrett, Esq. appeared for Plaintiff U.S. Merchant Systems LLC, a Delaware limited liabilty company.
Richard Fenn, the Chief Information Officer for plaintiff, was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Swedish Health Concepts, Inc., a/k/a Swedish Healthy Concept, a California Corporation, or John Masody, an individual.
Default was entered on June 27, 2008.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, November 24, 2008.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff U.S. Merchant Systems LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, and against the Defaulted Defendant Swedish Health Concepts Inc., a/k/a Swedish Healthy Concept, a California Corporation as follows:

Damages/Principal - $10,591.17.
Prejudgment Interest - $900.60.00.
Attorney's Fees - $21,532.00.
Costs - $2,537.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $35,560.77.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Default Prove-Up Hearing.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Evidence, Issue And/Or Terminating Sanctions Against Deft John Masody
Off Calendar Jan-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's Motion For Evidence, Issue, And/Or Terminating Sanctions Against Defendant John Masody is granted as it seeks evidentiary sanctions against Defendant John Masody. Defendant is barred from introducing any evidence at trial in support of any defenses he might have to Plaintiff's complaint. No opposition has been filed. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-28-2008 Assigned To Trial Court

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft'S Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Document;S
Off Calendar Dec-10-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
John Masody, Defendant appearing in Propria Persona

No other appearance. Tentative Ruling adopted: Plaintiff U.S. Merchant Systems, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Defendant's Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant is ordered to respond and to produce documents within 10 days of notice of this order. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $500, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
